mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, compressing equity risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0% within a single month.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling housing starts indicates a rotation from cyclical construction to durable goods manufacturing, which historically precedes equity outperformance over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change minus HOUST change) shows no predictive power for 60-day SPX returns across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects underlying consumer demand strength that directly supports corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth periods produce SPX forward returns indistinguishable from zero or negative over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An abrupt increase in the federal funds rate raises borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days when the hike exceeds historical norms.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in over 50% of months where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and rising unemployment claims indicate a structural mismatch in the labor market that suppresses wage-driven inflation expectations, supporting equity valuations over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and rising UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of observed instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in the industrial production index reflects capacity utilization improvements that lower unit labor costs and boost profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth periods show no statistically significant outperformance of SPX 60-day forward returns compared to contraction periods (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals weakening consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances following a 10% peak-to-trough decline in HOUST.
